step_description: Top SQL by CPU time and buffer gets from v$sql
rows: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id=:1 AND status=:2
SELECT p.name, SUM(oi.qty) FROM products p JOIN order_items
UPDATE customer_stats SET last_order_date=:1 WHERE id=:2
SELECT c.*, a.* FROM customers c JOIN accounts a ON c.id=a
narrative_hint: SQL 8fk2m9vbqt3dx is the dominant CPU consumer at 1,840 seconds with 4.8M buffer gets and 982K disk reads across 48,420 executions. The disk reads confirm a full table scan or large range scan. SQL 7tqm4xwjnb2dp is a heavy aggregate JOIN running 420 times consuming 2.8M buffer gets — likely a reporting query without proper indexes. The customer-account JOIN (rv2hs6ptmq8wc) shows 284K disk reads for 1,840 executions suggesting a large Cartesian-style join or missing foreign key index. Claude should prioritize sql_id 8fk2m9vbqt3dx and 7tqm4xwjnb2dp as the top two optimization targets.
